title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Wildfire smoke from Canada has caused yellowish skies and cooler temperatures in the Boston area.
The National Weather Service reports more smoke expected, likely limiting temperatures and causing hazy conditions.
Meteorologists expect the smoky conditions to persist through Wednesday, affecting visibility and air quality.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A Scottish traffic cone signed by Stuart Patrick arrived in Boston for a new sister city partnership with Glasgow.
The cone will symbolize Boston-Glasgow ties and support fundraising for mental health organizations in both regions.
The cone features illustrations celebrating both cities, and will be displayed at various Boston venues.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three MBTA employees were charged with overtime fraud and false track inspections at the Cabot Yard in Boston.
Brian Pfaffinger, Ronald Gamble, and others face conspiracy charges for falsifying inspection reports and working on personal vehicles.
Gamble submitted false timesheets for overtime with Pfaffinger's approval, while some employees allegedly slept during shifts.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston hosted seven World Cup games with France victorious in the final Foxborough match.
Boston's World Cup hosting cost taxpayers nearly $100 million for public transport improvements.
A Boston Olympic bid would require $4.7 billion for new venues, exceeding World Cup costs significantly.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jen Belichick, Bill Belichick's daughter-in-law, is wanted for arrest in North Carolina after missing a court appearance.
She was cited for speeding at 88 mph in a 70 mph zone in Brunswick County, North Carolina.
Bill Belichick currently coaches University of North Carolina's football team; Steve Belichick is the defensive coordinator.

title: Karen Read, Turtleboy look to get witnesses’ defamation suit tossed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read and Aidan Kearney seek dismissal of a defamation lawsuit from witnesses in Read's murder case.
Witnesses allege a campaign of defamation and harassment by Read and Kearney after public accusations in the 2022 case.
Read and Kearney filed motions using the anti-SLAPP law, arguing their actions were protected petitioning activities.

title: The invisible force helping fuel this summer’s intense heat, and all our weather
author: David Epstein
bullets: The jet stream plays a key role in this summer's intense heat across different regions, including Boston and Barcelona.
Quasi-resonant amplification may explain stalled weather systems, but its effects aren't fully proven, creating scientific debate.
Heat advisories extend from Montana to Maine due to the same atmospheric wave causing heat in various global locations.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Offensive line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' offensive line saw significant improvement in 2025 but remains a work in progress for 2026.
Jared Wilson has moved to center, with Alijah Vera-Tucker joining as the new left guard, replacing Wilson.
The Patriots' line ranked sixth in pass-block efficiency in 2025, but struggled with 21 postseason sacks allowed.

title: New mural brings fresh life to the corner of Doyle’s and the Midway Cafe
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A new mural of Barack Obama and Anthony Bourdain was unveiled in Jamaica Plain, inspired by their Vietnam meeting.
The mural is located between the former Doyle’s Cafe and Midway Cafe and aims to revive the area after Doyle's closure.
Artist Lee Beard, known as SOÉMS, created the mural which features quotes from both Obama and Bourdain.

title: Largest known bottlenose dolphin stranding hits Cape Cod
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Up to 30 bottlenose dolphins stranded off Cape Cod; several died, most refloated as tide returned.
Rescue crews monitored and herded dolphins away from shore; future strandings likely in coming days.
19 live dolphins found in three groups along nine-mile stretch; two tagged for monitoring.
